# Tip-recording Protocol v3

tags: #example

version: 3
updated: 2025-01-10
author: sample-user

---

## Purpose

Record single-fiber action potentials from lateral styloconic taste sensilla of Papilio xuthus larvae and quantify responses to bitter compounds.

---

## Reagents and Equipment

- Stimuli: Furanocoumarins (xanthotoxin, bergapten) each 1 mM in 0.1 mM KCl
- Recording electrode: Borosilicate glass capillary (outer diameter 1.0 mm), pulled (resistance 5–10 MΩ)
- Reference electrode: Ag/AgCl
- Amplifier: AC mode, high-pass filter 100 Hz, low-pass filter 3 kHz

## Procedure

1. Anesthetize larva on ice for 2 minutes
2. Fix dorsal side to platform with beeswax
3. Expose lateral styloconic sensilla and position under objective lens
4. Bring recording electrode into contact with the tip of the taste sensillum
5. Insert reference electrode into abdominal hemolymph
6. Fill electrode with stimulus solution and begin 10-second recording
7. Rinse with distilled water, then proceed to the next stimulus

## Validity Criteria

- Valid recording: stable baseline (SD < 0.05 mV), spike amplitude > 0.1 mV
- Invalid recording: electrode movement artifact, drift present

## Revision History

| Version | Change |
|---|---|
| v1 | Initial version |
| v2 | Stimulus concentration changed from 10 mM → 1 mM (to avoid saturated responses) |
| v3 | Anesthesia duration shortened from 5 min → 2 min (improved survival rate) |
